what_it_does: This file is a sent noticeboard item (channel message) authored by a claude_code session in the dragnet-development channel. It summarizes a proposed architecture fix for UI drift: treat the UI as typed composition from a registry and route every change through an automated pipeline, while planning a fork to build that architecture and keeping the current app stable.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, timestamps, and a filed→sent history entry.
The body diagnoses drift as repeated direct edits to one growing file.
It proposes a typed-composition model where surfaces, components, and actions are registered, contracted pieces assembled by the server.
It outlines a change-pipeline: understand intent → locate/reuse → implement as registry change → validate against contract → regenerate docs from registry → deploy live.
It claims the fix prevents crumbling via single source of truth, contracts, structural reuse, and derived docs.
It includes a plan: make this the fork's governing architecture, build registry + pipeline, re-home current app, and stop ad-hoc features meanwhile.
No links, no thread, no code symbols.
standouts: The proposal to stop hand-coding HTML and instead assemble the UI from a typed registry of surfaces/components/actions.
The explicit change-pipeline with reuse-first, fail-loud validation, docs regenerated from the registry, and live animated deploy.
The dual plan: fork builds the new architecture while the current app is frozen against further ad-hoc features.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter and markdown body.
Uses board:// address, ISO timestamps, and a history list for state transitions.
Title is truncated mid-sentence, likely by length limits.
No code symbols; purely prose communication.
concerns: Title is cut off ('You're dead right, and I wrote the idea up — it's in t'), so the referenced destination is incomplete.
The message references an external write-up/menu item that is not present in this file.
It makes claims about 'your own framework' and 'the mic' without local context.
Future-dated timestamp (2026) may be fictional/test data.
notes: A noticeboard message pitching a typed-composition registry plus change-pipeline as the fix for UI drift, and outlining a fork-and-stabilize plan.
questions: Where is the full write-up/menu item titled 'Interface architecture — typed composition + the change-pipeline'?
What is the current application and what does the 'mic' feature refer to?
Who is the intended recipient and what is the 'framework' already attributed to them?
What will the registry schema, contracts, and validation rules look like?
What exactly is the fork and how will the current app be re-homed onto the registry?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose is to communicate an architecture proposal and seek confirmation; the content matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: registry as single source of truth
intention_signals: It is trying to align the recipient around a structural diagnosis of UI drift and sell a registry/pipeline architecture as the durable fix, asking whether that matches the shape the recipient had in mind.
novelty: It reframes UI construction as server-assembled typed registry entries and every feature request as a pipeline-transformed registry change, with docs regenerated from the registry rather than maintained separately.
contribution: Contributes a concrete architecture proposal and rationale for a typed-composition registry with an end-to-end change-pipeline, plus a stabilization/fork plan intended to prevent further ad-hoc UI drift.
summary_for_embedding: A sent noticeboard message in dragnet-development proposing a typed-composition registry and automated change-pipeline to replace hand-coded UI edits, prevent drift, and govern a fork while keeping the current app stable.
intention_for_embedding: To communicate and validate an architecture proposal that stops ad-hoc UI edits by routing every change through a typed registry and pipeline.
